text: Heavy monsoon rains triggered severe flooding across central Thailand this week, submerging entire districts of Ayutthaya. Officials say more than 200,000 residents have been forced from their homes.

The floodwaters in Ayutthaya have damaged several historic temples and inundated two industrial parks, halting production at dozens of factories. Economic losses are expected to run into the billions.
Relief workers in central Thailand are struggling to reach stranded families as the flooding spreads south toward Bangkok. The government has opened emergency shelters for the displaced.
Thai authorities confirmed the flood death toll has risen to 45, with the hardest-hit provinces reporting widespread crop destruction across rice paddies.
A powerful magnitude 7.1 earthquake struck off the coast of northern Japan early Tuesday, collapsing buildings in the port city of Ishinomaki and cutting power to tens of thousands.
Rescue teams in Ishinomaki are digging through rubble after the earthquake, which authorities say killed at least 30 people and injured hundreds more.
The northern Japan earthquake damaged a section of coastal highway and forced the temporary shutdown of a nearby chemical plant as a precaution against leaks.
Aftershocks continued to rattle Ishinomaki on Wednesday as engineers assessed structural damage to the city's hospital, which was partially evacuated after the quake.
A fast-moving wildfire has scorched more than 40,000 acres of forest in northern California, destroying at least 150 homes in the town of Paradise Ridge.
Firefighters battling the Paradise Ridge blaze in California say strong winds are pushing the flames toward populated areas, prompting mandatory evacuations of three communities.
The California wildfire has claimed four lives and left dozens of residents unaccounted for, officials said, as smoke blanketed the region and closed several highways.
Power lines are being investigated as a possible cause of the Paradise Ridge wildfire, which has destroyed homes, a school, and a wine estate in northern California.
Hurricane Delia made landfall along the Gulf Coast of Louisiana on Saturday as a Category 3 storm, tearing roofs from houses in the town of Bayou Grand.
Storm surge from Hurricane Delia flooded coastal neighborhoods in Louisiana and knocked out power to more than half a million customers across the region.
Emergency crews in Bayou Grand are conducting water rescues after Hurricane Delia, which authorities blame for at least eight deaths along the Gulf Coast.
Hurricane Delia damaged an oil refinery and several shrimp docks in coastal Louisiana, dealing a heavy blow to the local fishing economy.
As the Thai flood crisis deepens, neighboring provinces have begun building sandbag barriers to protect Bangkok's outer suburbs from the advancing water.
Japanese officials raised the earthquake death toll to 38 on Thursday and warned that a damaged seawall near Ishinomaki could fail if another strong aftershock hits.
Containment of the Paradise Ridge wildfire reached 25 percent overnight as cooler weather aided crews, though officials cautioned that hundreds of structures remain threatened.
Residents of Bayou Grand returned to find their homes destroyed by Hurricane Delia, as Louisiana's governor requested a federal disaster declaration for the storm.
A charity concert raised funds for victims of the Thailand floods, whose damaged farmland and factories have left thousands without income.
Seismologists say the northern Japan earthquake ruptured a previously unmapped offshore fault, explaining the unexpectedly severe shaking in Ishinomaki.
Investigators confirmed the California wildfire began near a utility transmission tower, and the destroyed town of Paradise Ridge is now under a criminal review.
Cleanup from Hurricane Delia is expected to take months in coastal Louisiana, where the storm damaged homes, an oil refinery, and critical port infrastructure.
